search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/cg179 pressure area skin assessment risk pain colour change
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic repositioning, surface, dressing, offloading, referral, or treatment plan
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: No complete validated score inputs are present.
All prevention and management decisions remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults or children with a pressure-prone skin area or pressure-injury concern; mobility, neurologic, nutritional, device, critical-care, pediatric, and palliative contexts need qualified assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE home, primary, community, emergency, inpatient, rehabilitation, and wound-care review.
UAE_applicability: Assessment concepts are transferable, but UAE pressure-injury policy, equipment, nursing, wound, safeguarding, referral, and facility pathways remain required.
recency_verification: Current NICE CG179, last reviewed in 2018, was reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency limits remain.
superseded_check: NICE CG179 remains current and replaces CG29 and CG7.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact risk and skin-assessment section supports documenting a pressure-area review, onset or interval change, function and mobility impact, relevant risk context, clinician skin inspection, distribution or site, general appearance, pain, and previous records without assigning a risk score or diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact location, skin tone, mobility, devices, nutrition, moisture, sensation, risk tool, lesion dimensions, category, depth, infection, diagnosis, prevention plan, equipment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
